summary: The infant developing respiratory distress some time after birth is more indicative of HIVI/Respiratory Distress Syndrome compared to immediate onset or other conditions like those associated with antenatal steroids use. Term babies usually don't develop this condition right away; it’s often observed within days post-birth for preemies without preventive measures such as surfactant replacement therapy which might delay symptoms but doesn’t eliminate them entirely even if present at lower severity levels over longer periods due to underlying lung immaturity issues persisting despite treatment interventions aimed primarily towards preventing acute exacerbations caused by lack thereof initially leading up until full recovery occurs naturally given sufficient supportive care provided appropriately
